definition: The arising or originating condition of felt experience within dependent-arising analysis.
notes: Use this record when the origin-side compound is named compactly as vedana-samudaya rather than quoted as a fuller origin-line sentence. Keep the compound aligned with the headword defaults for vedana and samudaya, and do not let the term drift into emotion-language or generic feeling-language.

example_phrases.notes: Compact origin-side compound in the dependent-arising family.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use for the compact named vedana-samudaya compound inside the origin-side dependent-arising family; prefer fuller origin-line wording when the canonical sentence itself is present.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not flatten the compound into emotion-language or treat generic feeling-language as an interchangeable default when the repository's vedana policy is governing the passage.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the origin-side vedana compound from drifting into emotion-language or away from the repository's felt-experience default.
